terms.definition: Iterative optimization that updates parameters by moving opposite the gradient of a loss function, typically via θ ← θ − η∇L(θ).
Gradient descent where ∇L is computed using the entire training set before each parameter update; one update per epoch.
Synonym for batch gradient descent: the gradient averages over all training examples, not a mini-batch subset.
The positive scalar η in θ ← θ − η∇L(θ) that scales how far parameters move along the negative gradient each step.
One complete pass through the training procedure; in batch GD, one epoch corresponds to one full-batch gradient computation and one update.
